question: Is the image style realistic photography?
Is the camera perspective a wide-angle, low-angle shot looking upward?
Is the lighting a combination of soft light from a skylight and warm-toned spotlights?
Is a large dinosaur skeleton exhibit the dominant subject at the center of the frame?
Does the dinosaur skeleton span from left to right across the frame?
Is the dinosaur skull positioned in the upper right with jaws open?
Are the rib arcs clearly defined and the spine highlighted by spotlights?
Are exactly two rib sections in the middle of the skeleton made of semi-transparent acrylic material?
Do the acrylic rib sections display cool white luminous edges?
Is the floor light-colored stone reflecting the skeleton and spotlights?
Are there black stanchion posts with thin ropes forming a rectangular barrier around the skeleton?
Does one side of the barrier feature a low transparent panel with prominent reflections?
Is there an exhibit information placard standing tilted on a support stand inside the barrier on the left?
Is the placard face a solid color block (without visible text)?
Is there a glass display case containing rock fossils and a bone fragment on the left side?
Are there tiered seating benches, dark gray with matte surfaces, on the right side of the frame?
Is there a pure black, unlit interactive screen on a support bracket beside the benches?
Are there three visitors visible in the foreground/lower left area?
Is one adult visitor wearing a dark coat, standing and looking up at the skeleton?
Is there a child visitor wearing a light-colored hat half-crouching outside the barrier?
Is there an adult visitor pushing a stroller?
Is there a photographer in the middle right holding a camera aimed at the skeleton?
Is another smaller dinosaur skeleton visible in the far left background?
Is the background wall light beige with an arched structure?
